content.text:
REVIEW-FINDING: 5.1.1(i) WARN — Privacy policy states "We do not collect, process, or store your location data," but RunSessionManager.swift imports CoreLocation, calls requestWhenInUseAuthorization/startUpdatingLocation, and appends GPS route points (App/RunSessionManager.swift:1-15), and the app description advertises "maps every run with GPS."
Pierre: Apple's reviewers cross-check the privacy policy text against actual app behavior and metadata, and a direct contradiction like this is an easy rejection trigger under 5.1.1(i). Here the app clearly collects precise location via CLLocationManager and even markets GPS mapping as a core feature, yet the policy flatly denies any location collection. Fix the privacy policy to accurately disclose location collection/use/storage (including whether it's transmitted via iCloud sync) before submission.
